chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُنَّ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْإِمَاءِ وَ لَيْسَتْ مِنَ الْأَرْبَعِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – They (wives of Mut’a) are at the status of slave girls, and are not from the four (wives allowed)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/232)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/232)
text: ذَكَرْتُ لَهُ الْمُتْعَةَ أَ هِيَ مِنَ الْأَرْبَعِ فَقَالَ تَزَوَّجْ مِنْهُنَّ أَلْفاً فَإِنَّهُنَّ مُسْتَأْجَرَاتٌ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Is’haq, from Sa’dan Bin Muslim, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara, from his father,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I mentioned the Mut’a to himasws, is she from the four (allowable wives)?’ So heasws said: ‘You can take (by way of Mut’a) a thousand from them, so they are recompensed’.
en.sarwar: 7. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Ahmad ibn Ishaq from Sa‘dan ibn Muslim from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah from his father who has said the following: “I once mentioned al-Mut‘ah (advantageous marriage) before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and asked if it is one of four (wives). He (the Imam) said, ’You may marry a thousand of them; they are on hire. 5 ”
